economic_notes: COVID-19 impacted operations including office closures, travel restrictions, cancellation of events. Received additional ISC COVID funding. Reduced funding from Dakota Dunes Community Development Corporation and First Nations Trust due to SIGA casino closures. George Gordon Developments Limited Partnership (equity method) reported a net loss of $242,731 for year ended December 31, 2020. Gordon Retail Centre incorporated as 4 Claw Retail Inc. subsequent to year-end.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (year ended March 31, 2021). Province is Saskatchewan (Treaty 4 Territory). George Gordon Developments Limited Partnership is accounted for using the modified equity method (GBE). Six entities consolidated: George Gordon First Nation, George Gordon First Nation CMHC Housing, Gordon Retail Centre, George Gordon First Nation Off-Reserve Advisory Board Inc., GHawk Enterprises Ltd., George Gordon First Nation - TLE Revenue Account, and George Gordon First Nation TLE Holdings Ltd. George Gordon TLE Trust - Principal Trust Account excluded from consolidation as Chief and Council do not control the Trust. Prior year surplus of $11,359,242 includes a one-time $9,086,078 contribution of land and building from the TLE Trust Principal Trust Account. BHP Billiton accounts receivable of $800,000 is notable. Total government transfers estimated as ISC ($16,629,101) plus CMHC ($389,242) plus TCFS Prevention ($186,500) = $17,204,843 plus Touchwood Agency Tribal Council ($966,407) = $17,804,843; however Touchwood may be considered a transfer from a tribal council rather than government — included for completeness at $17,818,343 adding First Nations Trust ($813,617 partially government-sourced). OSR calculated as total revenue minus ISC and CMHC only for conservatism.
